_metadata.description: Toyota A3 / Gap Analysis applied to Canvas course design audits. The frame is Genchi Genbutsu — 'go and see for yourself' — and A3 structure (Current State → Target State → Gap → Root Cause → Countermeasure → Verification). Isolated vs systemic gaps. Output format for change plans.
_metadata.runtime_strategy_rationale: Body MD ~8,307 chars / 4 ≈ 2,077 tokens — within the 'either' band per runtime_strategy_rules. Set to read_at_runtime per named_override_patterns.selective_load: canvas_course_expert loads 2-3 of ~12 knowledge files per audit task. Selective-load defeats embed-cache benefits.

scope: The Toyota Production System / A3 Problem Solving methodology applied to Canvas course design audits. The single frame is Genchi Genbutsu (go and see) — the prerequisite to honest A3 work. The A3 structure (Current/Target/Gap/Root Cause/Countermeasure/Verification) is the artifact this frame produces. Boundaries: does not cover what to audit (see hattie_3phase, cognitive_load_theory, course_design_language); covers how to write up findings as a remediation plan.

principles.name: Genchi Genbutsu — Go and See for Yourself
principles.compact_statement: 現地現物. Don't make decisions from reports, dashboards, or theory. Go to gemba (the actual place where work happens) and observe the real work in real conditions. Toyota holds that A3 problem-solving cannot be done honestly without first going to gemba — you cannot accurately fill in Current State from a summary, Gap from intuition, or Root Cause from speculation. Each section requires firsthand observation. Applied to Canvas: gemba is the live course/ mirror (actual pages, module items, rubrics, student-visible titles). The audit agent MUST call parse_course_export() or read the course/ mirror via canvas_sync.py BEFORE proposing any A3. The principle in one line: an A3 written without going to gemba is a wish list; an A3 written after going to gemba is a plan.
principles.trigger: Before proposing any A3 entry on any Canvas finding. Producing a Current State line without having read the actual content is a Genchi Genbutsu violation — audit becomes theory dressed up as observation, and the Root Cause and Countermeasure downstream are unreliable as a result.
principles.trust_marker: Current State references the live course/ mirror by page/item name or canvas_id; Gap statement quotes or paraphrases observed content; Root Cause cites evidence from the mirror rather than general BYUI patterns or prior-semester memory. An A3 whose Current State and Gap could have been written without reading the actual course is a Genchi Genbutsu violation, regardless of how polished the six fields look. Worked examples in the MD body (Sprint 3 missing overview; Sprint 1 editor notes in titles) illustrate the principle applied to systemic and isolated gaps respectively.

sources: Toyota Production System / A3 Problem Solving methodology.
Applied to Canvas course design audits via canvas_course_expert.md.
provenance.provenance_requirement: optional (identity shape — the agent IS the source for how to apply the frame; original-source Toyota methodology documented for traceability)

pre_run_checklist: Each entry in consumed_by[] resolves to an existing agent spec
post_run_checklist: knowledge/toyota_gap_analysis_knowledge.json exists with shape=identity and principles[] present

changes: Retrofit: JSON companion authored for pre-existing canvas-toolbox knowledge MD.

Classified as framework-charter per knowledge_shapes._classification_guidance: shape=identity with principles[] containing ONE entry (genchi_genbutsu) representing the frame itself. The A3 structure (six fields) and worked examples (Sprint 3 missing overview, Sprint 1 editor notes) are illustrations in the MD body, not separate principles[] entries. This matches the behavioral_discipline pattern noted in the classification guidance: 'Toyota Genchi Genbutsu IS the single principle P-001; the worked examples illustrate the principle, they aren't separate principles.'

consumed_by inferred from 'Applied to: Canvas course design audits by canvas_course_expert.md' line at top of MD (canvas-toolbox header convention variant — 'Applied to:' equivalence-maps to 'Used by:').
Runtime strategy set to read_at_runtime via selective_load override.
